description: Massive multi-brand pop-up town across Seongsu, bringing online-only beauty brands to their first ever offline spaces.
research_insight: Proved an e-commerce platform could run a physical pop-up town as effectively as a department store.

description: Nike's Seoul-exclusive Air Force 1 drop with local artist collabs, customisation stations, and an interactive 'Seoul Streets' installation celebrating the city's sneaker culture.
tags: Nike | Air Force 1 | Sneakers | Seoul Edition | Custom
research_insight: Nike's city-edition drops have become annual cultural events — the Seongsu location ensures the brand speaks directly to Seoul's most trend-sensitive consumer segment.

description: Monthly curated vintage & resale pop-up reflecting MZ consumers' growing interest in sustainable fashion.
research_insight: Sustainable fashion is the fastest-growing MZ sub-trend — Hannam's market taps this with a premium approach.

description: Independent artist market selling handmade works, goods, and crafts — a defining feature of Hongdae's creative underground.
research_insight: The indie artist market represents the grassroots origin of Seoul's pop-up culture.

description: Hongdae's proximity to SM Town makes it a permanent K-pop corridor. Albums, photocards, lightsticks at every major comeback.
tags: K-Pop | Fandom | MD | SM Town | Ongoing
research_insight: Hongdae's role as K-pop's retail heartland is self-reinforcing: fans gather because brands pop up; brands pop up because fans gather.

description: Nine major beauty brands share one pop-up town. Visitors compare, trial, and purchase across all brands simultaneously.
research_insight: Coupang coordinating 9 brands shows how e-commerce platforms are emerging as pop-up town operators.

description: Fresh-food e-commerce platform Kurly brings curated brands offline with live tasting and cooking demos.
research_insight: Market Kurly's offline Festa bridges a core challenge for food e-commerce: consumers want to taste before buying.

description: The most successful pop-up of 2024: 250,000 total visitors, averaging 10,500 per day. Textbook cross-fandom collision.
tags: Cross-fandom | Record | Sanrio | K League | Sports
research_insight: Bridges two unconnected communities, doubling potential audience without increasing production complexity.

description: Major Korean and international webtoon IPs gather for a festival pop-up with author signings and story-world exhibitions.
research_insight: Webtoon IP generates deeply personal connections — fans follow characters for years, making purchases highly emotional.

description: Art and fashion hybrid pop-up at the iconic Daerim Changgo warehouse. Museum-style curation meets wearable art in a space that blurs gallery and retail.
research_insight: The Daerim Changgo venue — a repurposed factory — provides a backdrop that makes even simple installations feel monumental.

popup_name: Hightech Seoul Event at S Factory
description: Tech-art crossover exhibition at Seongsu's S Factory. Digital art installations, interactive tech experiences, and a curated selection of tech-adjacent lifestyle brands.
research_insight: S Factory's industrial scale allows for tech-art installations that smaller venues cannot accommodate — positioning Seongsu as a destination for digital culture.

description: Immersive Bulgaria rose concept space at iSOi's Seongsu flagship — a benchmark for brand-owned pop-up strategy with no rental costs.
research_insight: By owning the pop-up through their flagship, iSOi eliminated rental costs while generating enormous SNS buzz.

description: BLACKPINK's Jennie collaborates with experimental Seoul dessert brand NUDAKE for a pastry and beauty lifestyle pop-up at Haus Dosan, Apgujeong.
research_insight: NUDAKE's collab with Jennie bridges the worlds of K-pop fandom, luxury dessert culture, and lifestyle retail — a template for how celebrity IP can elevate an F&B brand.

description: Fashion meets coffee — a viral social media sensation before it even opened. Three-stripe drinks, limited merch, and brand installations drew massive queues.
research_insight: Pre-opening social buzz turned this into a must-visit — a case study in anticipation-building without paid advertising.

description: Running & trail shoe brand's pop-up with fit experience stations, limited-color drops, and stamp-tour giveaways.
research_insight: Hoka's shift from performance to lifestyle is embodied in Seongsu — targeting trend-aware consumers, not just runners.

description: Triple collab pop-up in Seongsu combining Musinsa's streetwear curation, Lacoste's preppy heritage, and Sanrio's character universe into one space.
research_insight: The Musinsa Empty format of triple collabs shows how multi-brand pop-ups can create cultural moments bigger than any single brand could alone.

popup_name: New Balance × Korean Webtoon Artist Pop-up
description: New Balance teams up with a Korean webtoon artist and marathon runner for a limited Seongsu pop-up celebrating running culture and local creativity.
tags: New Balance | Webtoon | Running | Collab | Art
research_insight: New Balance's sustained investment in running culture pop-ups in Seongsu mirrors the district's own transformation — both brand and neighbourhood have repositioned from functional to aspirational.

description: Hello Kitty 50th anniversary collab with co-designed limited apparel, accessories, and collectibles.
research_insight: The Hello Kitty 50th anniversary proved the staying power of legacy IP over trend-driven collabs.

description: Rotating platform for emerging Korean artists with works for sale and brand-collab art objects. Buyers receive limited-edition art books.
research_insight: Hannam's gallery infrastructure allows emerging artists to access affluent collectors without a permanent space.

description: Rotating art object and curiosity cabinet-style gallery pop-up in Hannam. Emerging Korean artists present collectible small-format works alongside collaborative brand objects.
tags: Gallery | Art Objects | Collectible | Emerging | Rotating
research_insight: The 'wunderkammer' (wonder cabinet) format — curating surprising juxtapositions of objects — is perfectly suited to Hannam's culture of discovery shopping.

description: First Korean pop-up for a coveted European niche perfume house. Personal fragrance consultations and limited discovery sets.
research_insight: Hannam's international-facing demographic is the ideal test market for European niche brands entering Korea.

description: Luxury Korean beauty and lifestyle brand Tamburins collaborates with Jennie of BLACKPINK. Object-art meets skincare in a gallery-style Hannam space.
tags: Tamburins | Jennie | BLACKPINK | Art Beauty | Gallery
research_insight: Tamburins's object-art approach to beauty retail — treating products as collectible sculptures — has inspired a wave of 'beauty as art' pop-ups across Hannam.

description: Blue Bottle Coffee's limited seasonal pop-up in Hannam featuring single-origin Korea-only blends and specialty equipment collaborations.
tags: Coffee | Blue Bottle | Specialty | Seasonal | Hannam
research_insight: Blue Bottle's minimalist aesthetic resonates with Hannam's design-literate consumer base — their pop-ups feel like concept cafés, not marketing events.

popup_name: Adidas × ABC Mart — "My Nth New Pair"
description: Season-launch pop-up combining ABC Mart's retail reach with Adidas' newest sneaker lineup and on-site shoe personalisation.
research_insight: Personalisation services dramatically increase time-in-store and purchase likelihood.

description: Premium interior and home brand pop-up offering product experience and consultation services.
research_insight: Home lifestyle brands use pop-ups to bridge the gap between e-commerce imagery and real-world texture.

popup_name: Olive Young Hongdae Town — Beauty Event
description: Multi-brand beauty event at Olive Young's Hongdae flagship. Makeup trials, new product demos, and SNS verification giveaways.
research_insight: Olive Young Hongdae's tourist-heavy foot traffic makes it one of the most cost-efficient locations for beauty brands.

description: Starbucks Reserve's Korea-exclusive seasonal pop-up in Hongdae celebrating Korean traditional flavours — yuzu, sikhye, and hojicha — in premium RTD and merchandise format.
tags: Starbucks | Coffee | Korea Heritage | Premium | Seasonal
research_insight: Starbucks Reserve Korea's seasonal pop-up format has mastered the art of creating product scarcity — limited tumblers sell out within hours, generating organic viral content.

description: Two of Korea's most respected streetwear brands — Covernat and Thisisneverthat — join forces for a Hongdae flagship collab pop-up featuring co-designed capsule pieces.
research_insight: When two credible Korean streetwear brands collab, the cultural weight is additive — both fanbases converge, creating a combined audience neither brand could reach alone.

description: Large-scale official anime pop-up with character goods, acrylic standees, apparel, and Korean-market collectibles. Long queues from day one.
research_insight: A 3-month run in a department store signals anime IP has become a consistent driver of retail footfall.

description: Korea's largest café & beverage expo with new F&B brand pop-ups, master barista demos, and specialty coffee showcases.
research_insight: The Café Show functions as an annual cultural moment for Seoul's café-obsessed MZ generation.

description: Korea's premier luxury herbal beauty brand Sulwhasoo transforms its Apgujeong flagship into an immersive hanok-inspired pop-up celebrating Korean botanical heritage.
goods: Heritage beauty sets + limited ginseng line
tags: Luxury Beauty | Sulwhasoo | Herbal | Hanok | Heritage
research_insight: Sulwhasoo's hanok-inspired pop-up design serves both domestic and international visitors — it positions K-beauty within Korea's broader cultural heritage narrative.

description: Experimental Korean fashion brand Ader Error's rotating pop-up series spanning Seongsu and Garosu-gil. Asymmetric cuts, oversized silhouettes, and spatial design that challenges retail conventions.
tags: Ader Error | Experimental Fashion | Capsule | Garosu-gil
research_insight: Ader Error defines what happens when fashion brand and spatial design merge — every pop-up is also an architecture statement.

description: K-pop supergroup NewJeans collabs with McDonald's Korea for a limited Gangnam pop-up café experience featuring character-themed meals and merchandise.
research_insight: The NewJeans × McDonald's Korea collab demonstrated that fast food brands can achieve luxury pop-up prestige through the right K-pop partnership.

description: Emerging designer market at the iconic DDP building with fashion, product design, and crafts curated by category.
research_insight: The DDP's Zaha Hadid landmark status gives any pop-up hosted there a cultural legitimacy no standard space can provide.

description: Coex Artium's rotating pop-up programme hosts Korean and international artists in the underground mall's gallery space, combining retail with cultural programming.
research_insight: Coex Artium's underground gallery space creates a captive audience of shoppers who become art viewers — one of Seoul's most effective art-retail hybrid formats.

description: Premium seasonal dessert pop-ups at Lotte Jamsil B1 bakery hall with season-limited pastry brands and holiday gift sets.
tags: Dessert | Gift Set | Seasonal | Jamsil | Bakery
research_insight: Department store bakery event halls are the most reliable pop-up format in Korea — low risk, high impulse purchase rate.

description: Paris Baguette's collab with the Louvre Museum brings art-themed pastries and Louvre-licenced merchandise to a Korean bakery pop-up experience.
tags: Bakery | Louvre | Art | Collab | Paris Baguette
research_insight: The Louvre × Paris Baguette collab is a masterclass in cultural licensing — it positions a mass-market Korean bakery brand within the prestige of French cultural heritage.

popup_name: SYSTEM FW25 Pop-up — Lotte World Mall
description: Korean contemporary fashion brand SYSTEM's FW25 collection launch pop-up with pre-order sessions and early access.
tags: Contemporary Fashion | FW25 | Pre-order | Korean Brand
research_insight: SYSTEM's use of Lotte World Mall expands its reach beyond Seongsu's fashion bubble to mainstream MZ consumers.

description: The iconic search-and-find character Wally comes to Seoul Forest's The Seoul Laitium. Interactive large-scale installations and limited Korean-market Wally merchandise.
research_insight: Classic character IPs like Wally generate cross-generational appeal — parents bring children, creating unusually long average visit times and higher per-group spending.

description: K-Beauty meets Pokémon — 1F Trend Fountain becomes a Pikachu & Minibu picnic with photo spots and themed beauty bundles.
research_insight: Shows how a beauty retailer can use IP licensing to create destination-worthy experiences.

description: Luxury French beauty pop-up with new collection launch, makeup experience zones, and exclusive YSL goods only available here.
research_insight: YSL entering Seongsu rather than Apgujeong signals the district has matured into a luxury brand destination.

description: Sports-themed sunscreen pop-up turning skincare education into a stadium experience. UV zones, SPF trials, photo installations.
research_insight: Turning skincare into a stadium concept shows brands competing for attention through narrative.

description: Science-based K-beauty brand Torriden opens a dedicated Seongsu pop-up showcasing its hyaluronic acid and barrier-care lineup with skin analysis stations.
research_insight: Torriden's data-driven skin analysis stations elevate the pop-up from a sales space into an educational experience — a growing trend in K-beauty retail.

description: Korean minimalist beauty brand Hince hosts a Seongsu pop-up featuring its signature matte lipsticks and new skincare line with curated pastel interiors.
research_insight: Hince's aesthetic-first approach to retail design makes its pop-ups photographable destinations — the brand understands that the space itself is the marketing.

description: French-inspired Korean fragrance and body care brand Lavoir opens a Seongsu pop-up with its signature laundry-aesthetic concept store.
tags: Fragrance | Body Care | French-inspired | Concept Store
research_insight: Lavoir's laundry-room aesthetic concept is immediately Instagram-worthy — proving that distinctive spatial design outperforms product-only marketing.

description: Viral handmade Japanese dango brand from Haengnidan-gil brings its signature chewy skewers to Seongsu for a two-month limited run.
tags: F&B | Japanese | Dango | Viral | Street Food
research_insight: Dango-ne's cult status in Haengnidan makes this Seongsu pop-up a destination for fans who missed the original — a perfect F&B cross-district expansion model.

description: French heritage brand celebrates 90+ years of the polo shirt through an immersive walk-through of construction, fabrics, and sustainability.
research_insight: Lacoste chose Seongsu over Gangnam — deliberate repositioning of a legacy brand toward MZ consumers.

description: Grand opening of Musinsa's flagship megastore with multi-brand pop-ups, exclusive drops, and opening celebration events.
research_insight: Musinsa's permanent megastore formalises Seongsu's evolution from pop-up hub to year-round destination.

description: Luxury Italian outerwear brand brings 'Puppy' summer collection in an art-forward exhibition format with seasonal limited pieces.
research_insight: Moncler's gallery-style format elevates brand experience — visitors engage as they would a gallery show.

popup_name: Nice Ghost Club × Cowboy Bebop Pop-up
description: Korean streetwear label Nice Ghost Club teams up with cult anime Cowboy Bebop. Graphic tees, accessories, and limited-run pieces reinterpreting Spike, Faye, and the Bebop crew.
tags: Streetwear | Cowboy Bebop | Anime | Collab | Limited
research_insight: Cowboy Bebop's global cult following combined with Nice Ghost Club's local credibility creates a rare crossover between Korean streetwear and classic anime aesthetics.

popup_name: Gentle Monster × Disney × F1 Circuit Collection
description: Eyewear brand Gentle Monster launches an F1-inspired collab with Disney. Limited circuit-edition sunglasses, immersive racing track installation, and exclusive photo moments.
tags: Gentle Monster | Disney | F1 | Eyewear | Collab
research_insight: Gentle Monster's flagship pop-ups are among Seoul's most architecturally impressive — this collab fuses motorsport culture with Disney's IP in an only-in-Seoul installation.

description: Enter a pink, squishy world where Ditto has copied everything — slides, plush toys, and décor. Part of Pokémon's 30th Anniversary.
research_insight: One of three simultaneous Pokémon events in Seongsu — Pokémon Mega Festa 2026 is unprecedented in scale.

brand: Pokémon Mega Festa × Olive Young N Seongsu
description: Birthday-party themed pop-up: cake, ribbon & balloon photo zones, coloring corner, and exclusive Birthday Edition merch.
research_insight: Olive Young's collab leverages Pokémon mania to drive flagship traffic — a perfect channel partnership model.

description: Two-story flagship: Floor 1 is a themed café with ice cream & drinks, Floor 2 is a full character merchandise floor.
research_insight: Two-floor café + shop format maximises dwell time — a growing blueprint for K-pop pop-ups.

description: World tour launching in Seoul first. Seoul-exclusive MD, new lightstick, plush, keyrings and keycaps. Continued in 20 cities worldwide.
research_insight: Seoul as global first stop confirms it as the world's most important pop-up market in 2026.

description: Official pop-up for NCT WISH with fan interaction zones, photo booths, and exclusive Seoul-edition merchandise.
tags: K-Pop | NCT | Fan Event | Seoul Exclusive
research_insight: SM chose Seongsu over SM Town Coex — reflecting the commercial appeal of Seongsu's younger foot traffic.

popup_name: Toy Story 'House of Toy Story' Pop-up
description: Pixar's Toy Story recreates a 1970s-80s American home across multiple floors. Photo zones with Woody, Buzz & Jessie, arcade-style games, DIY merch stations, and exclusive collectibles.
tags: Pixar | Toy Story | IP | Nostalgia | Photo Zone
research_insight: Toy Story's multi-generational appeal — beloved by both MZ parents and their children — makes it one of the broadest-reach IP pop-ups of 2026.

description: K-pop group (G)I-DLE's exhibition-format pop-up at Space S50 in Seongsu. Fan art installations, exclusive merchandise, and photocard events for NEVERLAND fans.
research_insight: Exhibition-format K-pop pop-ups are growing in sophistication — (G)I-DLE's art installations blur the line between fan merchandise event and gallery experience.

description: Experiential pop-up at T Factory Seongsu — latest Galaxy devices with hands-on trials and immersive tech zones.
research_insight: Tech brands using Seongsu's creative spaces signals the district's broad cultural credibility beyond fashion and beauty.

description: Niche fragrance brand Pesade opens its Hannam flagship with personal scent consultations and exclusive opening-day sets.
research_insight: Hannam's gallery culture makes it the natural home for niche fragrance brands seeking affluent, design-literate consumers.

popup_name: Gentle Monster Haus Dosan Flagship Experience
description: Gentle Monster's Haus Dosan flagship in Apgujeong runs rotating immersive installations. Part gallery, part eyewear store — each season brings a completely new spatial concept.
tags: Gentle Monster | Flagship | Gallery | Eyewear | Immersive
research_insight: Gentle Monster set the template for the 'retail as theatre' model that now defines Seoul's best pop-ups — every other brand is catching up to what Gentle Monster pioneered.

description: Amorepacific's derma care brand Aestura opens a Hannam pop-up with personalised skin consultation, barrier repair testing, and a limited slow-living product line.
research_insight: Derma-positioning in a Hannam lifestyle space rather than a pharmacy shifts brand perception from medical to aspirational — a strategic repositioning move.

description: Fwee's colour-focused K-beauty pop-up in Seongsu/Hongdae featuring their signature blush and lip products in a pastel-maximalist concept space.
research_insight: Fwee's colour-first brand identity translates naturally into an immersive pop-up environment — the products ARE the decoration.

description: Official pop-up tied to ITZY's Motto release with fan merch, photocard events, and Hongdae-only album bundles.
tags: K-Pop | ITZY | Fan Event | JYP | Hongdae Only
research_insight: Hongdae remains the spiritual home of K-pop fan culture — the natural first choice for comeback pop-ups.

description: The hit Japanese anime '3-nen Z-gumi Ginpachi-sensei' gets its first major Korean pop-up at Hongdae's AK Plaza. Character goods, classroom photo zones, and exclusive Korean-market merch.
tags: Anime | Japanese IP | Character | Hongdae | Golden Week
research_insight: Japanese anime IP pop-ups in Hongdae's AK Plaza have become a reliable fixture — the location's density of anime fans creates immediate queues on opening day.

description: Korean webtoon IP 'White Tiger and Black Tiger' gets a dedicated Hongdae pop-up with character goods, illustration prints, and fan meet events.
tags: Webtoon | Korean IP | Character | Fan Meet
research_insight: Korean webtoon IPs are increasingly commanding the same pop-up energy as Japanese anime — a shift reflecting the growing global reach of the webtoon format.

description: Spring-season dessert pop-up in Gangnam with seasonal pastries and cherry blossom period limited menus.
research_insight: Seasonal F&B pop-ups timed to cherry blossom season consistently outperform in foot traffic.

description: Unique sand art experience pop-up at Yeouido. Visitors create personalised sand art bottles and watch live sand animation performances.
tags: Art Experience | Sand | Yeouido | Interactive | Craft
research_insight: Experience-over-product pop-ups like Sand Museum have the highest word-of-mouth spread — the product (your sand bottle) becomes a walking advertisement.

description: Sanrio's Hello Kitty collabs with BLACKPINK's Jisoo. Co-designed fashion pieces, limited character goods, and signature photo zones.
tags: Sanrio | BLACKPINK | Jisoo | Collab | Golden Week
research_insight: Combining Hello Kitty with Jisoo targets two overlapping fandoms simultaneously — executed during peak visitor season.

description: Outdoor Pokémon pop-up where hidden Pokémon lurk among Seoul Forest trees, tied to the 2026 Seoul International Garden Expo.
tags: Pokémon | Outdoor | Seoul Forest | Garden Expo
research_insight: Taking a pop-up outdoors into Seoul Forest transforms the experience from retail into a nature walk.

description: Adorable honey-coloured bear character Butter Bear gets a dedicated Gangnam pop-up with plush goods, café-style drinks, and photo zones.
tags: Character | Bear | Cute | Café | Golden Week
research_insight: Bear character IPs have dominated Korean pop-up culture since BT21's Shooky and RJ — Butter Bear's warm colour palette appeals to the aesthetic-first Gen Z consumer.

description: BeautyPlus's mobile pop-up at Sungshin Women's University in collaboration with hair care brand Mise-en-scène.
tags: Hair Care | Mobile Pop-up | Campus | University
research_insight: Campus-based beauty pop-ups target the MZ demographic at point of brand discovery.

description: Viral Chinese-style dumpling brand Guomanduzi opens a pop-up in Anyang during Golden Week 2026, bringing its signature pan-fried dumplings to the greater Seoul area.
research_insight: Viral F&B pop-ups expanding from central Seoul to Gyeonggi satellite cities signal the format's democratisation — pop-up culture is no longer exclusively a Seoul phenomenon.

description: Children's outdoor lifestyle brand Jeep Kids pops up at Yeongdeungpo IFC Mall with miniature adventure gear, customisation stations, and family photo zones.
research_insight: Outdoor lifestyle brands targeting children in pop-up format are capitalising on post-pandemic parents' emphasis on active family experiences.

description: Nintendo's Super Mario franchise at Starfield Hanam with interactive game-themed installations and limited merchandise.
research_insight: Nintendo's strategic use of Golden Week maximises family traffic at a suburban location.

description: K-Pop group TOURS at Yongsan iPark Mall with fan merch, exclusive Yongsan-edition goods, and photocard events.
tags: K-Pop | Fan Event | Yongsan | Golden Week
research_insight: Yongsan's proximity to major transit hubs makes it accessible to fans travelling from across the country.

description: Korean character brand Nidi Girl collaborates with Gwangjin-gu café Overdose for a limited character-themed café pop-up with exclusive Nidi Girl drinks and goods.
tags: Character | Café | Collab | Korean IP | Gwangjin
research_insight: Character café collabs in residential neighbourhoods like Gwangjin represent the suburbanisation of Seoul's pop-up culture — moving beyond the Seongsu/Hannam axis.

description: Children's character brand JB Kids opens a pop-up at Yeongdeungpo IFC Mall targeting young families with interactive play zones and character merchandise.
tags: Kids | Character | Family | IFC Mall | Yeongdeungpo
research_insight: Pop-ups targeting children at IFC Mall represent a strategic shift — brands are investing in the next generation of consumers while capturing today's family spending.
